---
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    formats: ipynb,Rmd
    text_representation:
      extension: .Rmd
      format_name: rmarkdown
      format_version: '1.2'
      jupytext_version: 1.19.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: birder
    language: python
    name: birder
---

```{python}
import json
import os
import re
from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
import torch
```

```{python}
# %cd ..
```

```{python}
from birder.datahub.evaluation import AwA2
from birder.datahub.evaluation import CaltechCameraTraps
from birder.datahub.evaluation import NeWT
from birder.eval._embeddings import load_embeddings
from birder.eval.benchmarks import awa2
from birder.eval.benchmarks import cct
from birder.eval.benchmarks import newt
```

### Progress

```{python}
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BenchmarkConfig:
    title: str
    metric: str
    results_path: Path
    embeddings_column: str = "embeddings_file"
```

```{python}
EPOCH_RE = re.compile(r"_e(\d+)(?=_|$)")
MODEL_SUFFIX_RE = re.compile(r"(?:_(?:e\d+|\d+))?_\d+px_crop[\d.]+(?:_\d+)?(?:_sc)?_embeddings$")

MODEL_LABELS = {
    "vit_reg4_so150m_p14_ls_dino-v2-bio": "BIO-DINO",
    # References
    "convnext_v1_large_fb-imagenet22k": "ConvNeXt Large",
    "rope_cs_vit_reg4_b16_nape_ls_c1_dino-v3-lvd1689m": "DINOv3 B16",
    "rope_cs_vit_reg4_l16_nape_ls_c1_dino-v3-lvd1689m": "DINOv3 L16",
    "rope_i_vit_b16_pn_aps_c1_pe-core": "PE-Core B16",
    "rope_i_vit_l14_pn_aps_c1_pe-core": "PE-Core L14",
    "vit_b16_pn_bioclip-v1": "BioCLIP v1 B16",
    "vit_l14_pn_bioclip-v2": "BioCLIP v2 L14",
    "vit_b16_pn_biotrove-clip-o": "BioTrove B16",
    "vit_l14_pn_quick_gelu_openai-clip": "OpenAI CLIP L14",
    "vit_reg4_b14_nps_ls_dino-v2-lvd142m": "DINOv2 B14",
    "vit_reg4_l14_nps_ls_dino-v2-lvd142m": "DINOv2 L14",
    "vit_so400m_p14_ap_siglip-v2-webli": "SigLIP 2 SO400M",
}
METRIC_NAMES = {
    "ami_score": "Adjusted Mutual Information",
    "macro_f1": "Macro F1 Score",
    "val_macro_f1": "Macro F1 Score",
    "accuracy": "Accuracy",
    "val_accuracy": "Accuracy",
    "test_accuracy": "Accuracy",
    "k_1_acc": "k-NN (k=1) Accuracy",
    "k_3_acc": "k-NN (k=3) Accuracy",
    "k_5_acc": "k-NN (k=5) Accuracy",
    "k_10_acc": "k-NN (k=10) Accuracy",
    "k_100_acc": "k-NN (k=100) Accuracy",
    "k_10_cross_location_acc": "k-NN (k=10) Cross-location Accuracy",
    "2_shot_acc": "2 Shot Accuracy",
    "5_shot_acc": "5 Shot Accuracy",
    "mean_average_precision": "mAP",
}
BENCHMARK_CONFIG = {
    "awa2": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="Animals with Attributes 2",
        metric="macro_f1",
        results_path=Path("results/awa2/awa2_mlp_metric-present-only_runs3_e100_bs128_lr0p0001_hd512_do0p5.csv"),
    ),
    "bioscan5m": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="BIOSCAN-5M",
        metric="ami_score",
        results_path=Path("results/bioscan5m/bioscan5m_ami_umap50_l2-on.csv"),
    ),
    "butterflies": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="Butterflies",
        metric="val_macro_f1",
        results_path=Path("results/butterflies/butterflies_knn_k10.csv"),
    ),
    "cct": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="Caltech Camera Traps",
        metric="k_10_cross_location_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/cct/cct_split-main_k10-20-100.csv"),
    ),
    "cub200": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="CUB-200-2011",
        metric="mean_average_precision",
        results_path=Path("results/cub200/cub200_retrieval_k1-5-10.csv"),
    ),
    "fishnet": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="FishNet",
        metric="macro_f1",
        results_path=Path("results/fishnet/fishnet_mlp_runs3_e100_bs128_lr0p0001_hd512_do0p5.csv"),
    ),
    "flowers102": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="Flowers-102",
        metric="test_accuracy",
        results_path=Path("results/flowers102/flowers102_simpleshot.csv"),
    ),
    "fungiclef": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="FungiCLEF",
        metric="k_3_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/fungiclef/fungiclef_knn_k1-3_runs5.csv"),
    ),
    "imagenet1k": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="ImageNet-1K",
        metric="k_100_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/imagenet1k/imagenet1k_simpleshot_k5-10-100_runs5.csv"),
        embeddings_column="train_embeddings_file",
    ),
    "nabirds": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="NABirds",
        metric="k_10_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/nabirds/nabirds_knn_k10-20-100.csv"),
    ),
    "newt": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="NeWT",
        metric="accuracy",
        results_path=Path("results/newt/newt_svm_runs3_niter100.csv"),
    ),
    "plankton": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="Plankton",
        metric="accuracy",
        results_path=Path("results/plankton/plankton_linear_runs3_e50_bs64_lr0p002.csv"),
    ),
    "plantdoc": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="PlantDoc",
        metric="2_shot_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/plantdoc/plantdoc_simpleshot_k2-5_runs5.csv"),
    ),
    "plantnet": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="PlantNet",
        metric="2_shot_acc",
        results_path=Path("results/plantnet/plantnet_simpleshot_k2-5_runs3.csv"),
    ),
    "snakeclef": BenchmarkConfig(
        title="SnakeCLEF",
        metric="accuracy",
        results_path=Path("results/snakeclef/snakeclef_linear_runs3_e50_bs512_lr0p001.csv"),
    ),
}
```

```{python}
def pretty_name(name: str, dataset_name: str) -> str:
    key = name.removeprefix(f"{dataset_name}_")
    key = MODEL_SUFFIX_RE.sub("", key)
    key = re.sub(r"(?:_sc)?_embeddings$", "", key)

    return MODEL_LABELS.get(key, key.replace("_", " "))


def build_plot_data(
    df: pl.DataFrame, embeddings_column: str, metric_column: str
) -> tuple[str, list[tuple[int, float]], list[tuple[str, float]]]:
    series: list[tuple[int, float]] = []
    references: list[tuple[str, float]] = []
    for emb_path, metric in df.select(embeddings_column, metric_column).iter_rows():
        name = Path(emb_path).name
        match = EPOCH_RE.search(name)

        if match is None:
            references.append((Path(name).stem, float(metric)))
            continue

        epoch = int(match.group(1))
        key = EPOCH_RE.sub("", Path(name).stem)
        series.append((epoch, float(metric)))

    return key, sorted(series), references


def plot_epoch_curve_vs_references(
    epochs: list[int],
    metrics: list[float],
    main_label: str,
    reference_rows: list[dict],
    reference_score_key: str,
    ylabel: str,
    title: str,
) -> None:
    with plt.style.context("seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid"):
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8), facecolor="#F8F5EF")
        ax.set_facecolor("#FCFBF8")
        ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
        ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)

        ax.plot(epochs, metrics, marker="o", label=main_label)

        cmap = plt.get_cmap("tab10")
        for i, row in enumerate(reference_rows):
            ax.axhline(
                row[reference_score_key],
                color=cmap(i),
                linestyle=(0, (2.5, 4)),
                linewidth=1.1,
                alpha=0.9,
                label=row["model_label"],
            )

        # Axes
        ax.set_xlabel("Epoch", fontsize=12)
        ax.set_ylabel(ylabel, fontsize=12)

        min_epoch = min(epochs)
        max_epoch = max(epochs)
        x_padding = max(4, int((max_epoch - min_epoch) * 0.1))
        ax.set_xlim(min_epoch, max_epoch + x_padding)

        # Legend
        handles, labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
        legend_entries = [(metrics[-1], handles[0], labels[0])]
        legend_entries += [
            (row[reference_score_key], h, l) for row, h, l in zip(reference_rows, handles[1:], labels[1:])
        ]
        legend_entries.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)

        ax.legend(
            [h for _, h, _ in legend_entries],
            [l for _, _, l in legend_entries],
            loc="center left",
            bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 0.5),
            frameon=False,
            fontsize=10,
            handlelength=2.8,
        )

        # Rank
        final_metric = metrics[-1]
        position = 1 + sum(row[reference_score_key] > final_metric for row in reference_rows)
        total = len(reference_rows) + 1
        ax.text(
            1.0,
            1.01,
            f"Rank: {position}/{total}",
            transform=ax.transAxes,
            fontsize=11,
            ha="right",
            va="bottom",
            fontweight="bold",
        )

        # Title
        ax.set_title(title, fontsize=18, fontweight="bold", color="#262320", pad=18)

        fig.tight_layout(rect=(0, 0, 0.85, 1))

    plt.show()
    plt.close(fig)
```

```{python}
ScoreMethod = Callable[[pl.DataFrame], pl.DataFrame]


def build_benchmark_rows(df: pl.DataFrame, benchmark: str, cfg: BenchmarkConfig) -> pl.DataFrame:
    metric_col = cfg.metric
    emb_col = cfg.embeddings_column

    rows = []
    for emb_path, metric in df.select(emb_col, metric_col).iter_rows():
        stem = Path(emb_path).stem
        metric = float(metric)
        epoch_match = EPOCH_RE.search(stem)

        if epoch_match is None:
            rows.append(
                {
                    "benchmark": benchmark,
                    "benchmark_title": cfg.title,
                    "kind": "reference",
                    "model_key": stem,
                    "model_label": pretty_name(stem, benchmark),
                    "epoch": None,
                    "raw_score": metric,
                }
            )
            continue

        epoch = int(epoch_match.group(1))
        model_key = EPOCH_RE.sub("", stem)

        rows.append(
            {
                "benchmark": benchmark,
                "benchmark_title": cfg.title,
                "kind": "train",
                "model_key": model_key,
                "model_label": pretty_name(model_key, benchmark),
                "epoch": epoch,
                "raw_score": metric,
            }
        )

    return pl.DataFrame(rows)


def score_ref_minmax_clipped(rows_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    ref_df = rows_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "reference")
    ref_min = ref_df["raw_score"].min()
    ref_max = ref_df["raw_score"].max()
    ref_range = ref_max - ref_min

    if ref_range == 0:
        return rows_df.with_columns(
            pl.lit(ref_min).alias("ref_min"),
            pl.lit(ref_max).alias("ref_max"),
            pl.lit(ref_range).alias("ref_range"),
            pl.lit(1.0).alias("score"),
        )

    return rows_df.with_columns(
        pl.lit(ref_min).alias("ref_min"),
        pl.lit(ref_max).alias("ref_max"),
        pl.lit(ref_range).alias("ref_range"),
        ((pl.col("raw_score") - ref_min) / ref_range).clip(0.0, 1.0).alias("score"),
    )


def score_ref_minmax_raw(rows_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    ref_df = rows_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "reference")
    ref_min = ref_df["raw_score"].min()
    ref_max = ref_df["raw_score"].max()
    ref_range = ref_max - ref_min

    if ref_range == 0:
        return rows_df.with_columns(
            pl.lit(ref_min).alias("ref_min"),
            pl.lit(ref_max).alias("ref_max"),
            pl.lit(ref_range).alias("ref_range"),
            pl.lit(1.0).alias("score"),
        )

    return rows_df.with_columns(
        pl.lit(ref_min).alias("ref_min"),
        pl.lit(ref_max).alias("ref_max"),
        pl.lit(ref_range).alias("ref_range"),
        ((pl.col("raw_score") - ref_min) / ref_range).alias("score"),
    )


def score_relative_to_best(rows_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    ref_df = rows_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "reference")
    ref_max = ref_df["raw_score"].max()

    return rows_df.with_columns(
        pl.lit(ref_max).alias("ref_max"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") / ref_max).alias("score"),
    )


def score_rank_among_refs(rows_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    ref_scores = rows_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "reference")["raw_score"].to_list()
    n_refs = len(ref_scores)

    return rows_df.with_columns(
        pl.struct(["raw_score"])
        .map_elements(
            lambda x: sum(s <= x["raw_score"] for s in ref_scores) / n_refs,
            return_dtype=pl.Float64,
        )
        .alias("score")
    )


def build_scores_df(method: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    score_fn = SCORING_METHODS[method]

    scored_parts = []
    for benchmark, cfg in BENCHMARK_CONFIG.items():
        df = pl.read_csv(cfg.results_path)
        rows_df = build_benchmark_rows(df, benchmark, cfg)
        scored_parts.append(score_fn(rows_df))

    return pl.concat(scored_parts, how="vertical")


def build_epoch_progress_df(scores_df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    train_df = scores_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "train").sort(["benchmark", "epoch"])

    train_with_deltas = train_df.with_columns(
        pl.col("score").shift(1).over("benchmark").alias("prev_score"),
        pl.col("raw_score").shift(1).over("benchmark").alias("prev_raw_score"),
    ).with_columns(
        (pl.col("score") - pl.col("prev_score")).alias("score_delta"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") - pl.col("prev_raw_score")).alias("raw_score_delta"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") > pl.col("prev_raw_score")).alias("improved_vs_prev"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") < pl.col("prev_raw_score")).alias("declined_vs_prev"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") == pl.col("prev_raw_score")).alias("unchanged_vs_prev"),
        (pl.col("raw_score") == pl.col("raw_score").cum_max().over("benchmark")).alias("is_best_so_far"),
    )

    epoch_progress_df = (
        train_with_deltas.group_by("epoch")
        .agg(
            pl.col("score").mean().alias("mean_score"),
            pl.col("score").median().alias("median_score"),
            pl.col("score_delta").mean().alias("mean_score_delta"),
            pl.col("raw_score_delta").mean().alias("mean_raw_score_delta"),
            pl.col("improved_vs_prev").sum().alias("n_improved"),
            pl.col("declined_vs_prev").sum().alias("n_declined"),
            pl.col("unchanged_vs_prev").sum().alias("n_unchanged"),
            pl.col("is_best_so_far").sum().alias("n_best_so_far"),
            pl.col("benchmark").n_unique().alias("n_benchmarks"),
            pl.col("model_label").first().alias("model_label"),
        )
        .sort("epoch")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("n_improved") / pl.col("n_benchmarks")).alias("improvement_rate"),
            pl.col("mean_score").cum_max().alias("best_mean_score_so_far"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("best_mean_score_so_far") - pl.col("mean_score")).alias("gap_to_best_mean_score"),
        )
    )

    return train_with_deltas, epoch_progress_df


def build_reference_agg_df(scores_df: pl.DataFrame, min_benchmarks: int = 8) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        scores_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "reference")
        .group_by("model_label")
        .agg(
            pl.col("score").mean().alias("mean_score"),
            pl.col("benchmark").n_unique().alias("n_benchmarks"),
        )
        .filter(pl.col("n_benchmarks") >= min_benchmarks)
        .sort("mean_score", descending=True)
    )
```

```{python}
benchmark = "cub200"
cfg = BENCHMARK_CONFIG[benchmark]

df = pl.read_csv(cfg.results_path)
dataset_name = cfg.results_path.stem.split("_")[0]
metric_title = METRIC_NAMES[cfg.metric]

label, points, references = build_plot_data(df, cfg.embeddings_column, cfg.metric)
epochs, metrics = zip(*points)

reference_rows = [
    {"model_label": pretty_name(ref_label, dataset_name), "score": ref_metric} for ref_label, ref_metric in references
]

plot_epoch_curve_vs_references(
    epochs=list(epochs),
    metrics=list(metrics),
    main_label=pretty_name(label, dataset_name),
    reference_rows=reference_rows,
    reference_score_key="score",
    ylabel=metric_title,
    title=f"{cfg.title} Benchmark",
)
```

```{python}
SCORING_METHODS: dict[str, ScoreMethod] = {
    "ref_minmax_clipped": score_ref_minmax_clipped,
    "ref_minmax_raw": score_ref_minmax_raw,
    "relative_to_best": score_relative_to_best,
    "rank_among_refs": score_rank_among_refs,
}

score_method = "ref_minmax_raw"
exclude_benchmarks = {"imagenet1k"}
# exclude_benchmarks = exclude_benchmarks.union({"nabirds", "cub200"})

scores_df = build_scores_df(score_method).filter(~pl.col("benchmark").is_in(exclude_benchmarks))

# See if any "runs" are missing
epoch_counts = (
    scores_df.filter(pl.col("kind") == "train").group_by("benchmark").agg(pl.col("epoch").n_unique().alias("n_epochs"))
)
max_epochs = epoch_counts["n_epochs"].max()
epoch_counts.filter(pl.col("n_epochs") < max_epochs)
```

```{python}
train_scores_df, epoch_progress_df = build_epoch_progress_df(scores_df)
ref_agg_df = build_reference_agg_df(scores_df, min_benchmarks=8)

reference_rows = list(ref_agg_df.iter_rows(named=True))

plot_epoch_curve_vs_references(
    epochs=epoch_progress_df["epoch"].to_list(),
    metrics=epoch_progress_df["mean_score"].to_list(),
    main_label=epoch_progress_df["model_label"][0],
    reference_rows=reference_rows,
    reference_score_key="mean_score",
    ylabel=f"Mean Score ({score_method})",
    title=f"Aggregate Benchmark Score - {score_method}",
)
```

```{python}
with plt.style.context("seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid"):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    ax.plot(
        epoch_progress_df["epoch"].to_list(),
        epoch_progress_df["improvement_rate"].to_list(),
        marker="o",
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("Improvement Rate")
    ax.set_title("Fraction of Benchmarks Improved vs Previous Epoch")
    plt.show()
    plt.close(fig)
```

```{python}
total_benchmarks = int(epoch_progress_df["n_benchmarks"].max())
with plt.style.context("seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid"):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))
    ax.plot(
        epoch_progress_df["epoch"].to_list(),
        epoch_progress_df["n_best_so_far"].to_list(),
        marker="o",
    )
    ax.axhline(
        total_benchmarks,
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=1.5,
        color="gray",
        label=f"Total benchmarks ({total_benchmarks})",
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("# Benchmarks at Best-so-far")
    ax.set_title("Benchmarks Hitting Best-so-far at Each Epoch")
    plt.show()
    plt.close(fig)
```

### Mistakes

```{python}
def _show_image(ax, image_path: str) -> None:
    if image_path and os.path.exists(image_path):
        ax.imshow(plt.imread(image_path))
    else:
        ax.text(0.5, 0.5, "Image not found", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8)

    ax.axis("off")


def plot_mistake_grid(
    rows: list[dict],
    start: int,
    n: int,
    cols: int,
    suptitle_template: str,
    title_fn,
    *,
    fig_scale: float = 3.0,
    title_fontsize: int = 7,
) -> None:
    batch = rows[start : start + n]
    if len(batch) == 0:
        return

    n_rows = int(np.ceil(len(batch) / cols))
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, cols, figsize=(cols * fig_scale, n_rows * fig_scale))
    axes = np.atleast_1d(axes).ravel()

    for ax, row in zip(axes, batch):
        _show_image(ax, str(row.get("image_path", "")))
        ax.set_title(title_fn(row), fontsize=title_fontsize)

    for ax in axes[len(batch) :]:
        ax.axis("off")

    end = start + len(batch)
    fig.suptitle(suptitle_template.format(start=start + 1, end=end, total=len(rows)), fontsize=12)
    plt.tight_layout()
```

```{python}
DEVICE = torch.device("cuda:1")
```

### AwA2

```{python}
AWA2_DATASET_PATH = "~/Datasets/Animals_with_Attributes2"
AWA2_EMBEDDINGS_PATH = (
    "results/awa2/awa2_vit_reg4_so150m_p14_ls_dino-v2-bio_0_e150_224px_crop1.0_37322_sc_embeddings.parquet"
)
AWA2_SEED = 0
AWA2_EPOCHS = 100
AWA2_BATCH_SIZE = 256
```

```{python}
awa2_dataset = AwA2(os.path.expanduser(AWA2_DATASET_PATH))
awa2_metadata_df, awa2_attribute_matrix, awa2_class_names, awa2_train_classes, awa2_test_classes = (
    awa2._load_awa2_metadata(awa2_dataset)
)
awa2_x_train, awa2_y_train, awa2_x_test, awa2_y_test = awa2._load_embeddings_with_labels(
    AWA2_EMBEDDINGS_PATH,
    awa2_metadata_df,
    awa2_attribute_matrix,
    awa2_class_names,
    awa2_train_classes,
    awa2_test_classes,
)
```

```{python}
awa2_model = awa2.train_mlp(
    awa2_x_train,
    awa2_y_train,
    num_classes=len(awa2_dataset.attribute_names),
    device=DEVICE,
    epochs=AWA2_EPOCHS,
    batch_size=AWA2_BATCH_SIZE,
    lr=1e-4,
    hidden_dim=512,
    dropout=0.5,
    seed=AWA2_SEED,
)
awa2_y_pred, awa2_y_true, _ = awa2.evaluate_mlp(
    awa2_model,
    awa2_x_test,
    awa2_y_test,
    batch_size=AWA2_BATCH_SIZE,
    device=DEVICE,
)
```

```{python}
awa2_present_attrs = np.where(awa2_y_true.sum(axis=0) > 0)[0]
awa2_emb_ids_df = load_embeddings(AWA2_EMBEDDINGS_PATH).select(["id"])

awa2_test_meta = (
    awa2_metadata_df.join(awa2_emb_ids_df, on="id", how="inner")
    .filter(pl.col("class_name").is_in(awa2_test_classes))
    .with_columns(
        pl.concat_str(
            pl.lit(f"{awa2_dataset.images_dir}/"),
            pl.col("class_name"),
            pl.lit("/"),
            pl.col("id"),
            pl.lit(".jpg"),
        ).alias("image_path")
    )
    .select(["class_name", "image_path"])
)

assert awa2_test_meta.height == awa2_y_true.shape[0], "AwA2 metadata rows are not aligned with prediction vector length"
```

```{python}
awa2_rng = np.random.default_rng(AWA2_SEED)
awa2_test_rows = awa2_test_meta.to_dicts()
awa2_attr_acc = (awa2_y_pred == awa2_y_true).mean(axis=0)
awa2_mistakes: list[dict] = []

for attr_idx in awa2_present_attrs:
    mismatch = np.flatnonzero(awa2_y_pred[:, attr_idx] != awa2_y_true[:, attr_idx])
    if mismatch.size == 0:
        continue

    sample_idx = int(awa2_rng.choice(mismatch))
    assert (
        awa2_y_pred[sample_idx, attr_idx] != awa2_y_true[sample_idx, attr_idx]
    ), "AwA2 sampled row is not a real mistake"

    row = awa2_test_rows[sample_idx]
    actual_value = int(awa2_y_true[sample_idx, attr_idx])
    predicted_value = int(awa2_y_pred[sample_idx, attr_idx])
    awa2_mistakes.append(
        {
            "attribute": awa2_dataset.attribute_names[attr_idx],
            "actual_label": "True" if actual_value == 1 else "False",
            "predicted_label": "True" if predicted_value == 1 else "False",
            "accuracy": float(awa2_attr_acc[attr_idx]),
            "class_name": row["class_name"],
            "image_path": row["image_path"],
        }
    )

print(f"Found mistakes for {len(awa2_mistakes)}/{len(awa2_present_attrs)} present attributes")
# pl.DataFrame(awa2_mistakes).head(10)
```

```{python}
def plot_awa2_mistakes(rows: list[dict], start: int, n: int, cols: int) -> None:
    plot_mistake_grid(
        rows,
        start,
        n,
        cols,
        suptitle_template="AwA2 mistakes: one per attribute ({start}-{end} / {total})",
        title_fn=lambda row: (
            f"{row['attribute']}\n"
            f"Actual: {row['actual_label']} | Predicted: {row['predicted_label']}\n"
            f"Class: {row['class_name']} | Attr acc: {row['accuracy']:.1%}"
        ),
        fig_scale=2.8,
        title_fontsize=8,
    )


plot_awa2_mistakes(awa2_mistakes, start=0, n=20, cols=5)
```

### NeWT

```{python}
NEWT_DATASET_PATH = "~/Datasets/NeWT"
NEWT_EMBEDDINGS_PATH = (
    "results/newt/newt_vit_reg4_so150m_p14_ls_dino-v2-bio_0_e150_224px_crop1.0_36032_sc_embeddings.parquet"
)
NEWT_N_ITER = 30
NEWT_N_JOBS = 8
NEWT_SEED = 0
```

```{python}
newt_dataset = NeWT(os.path.expanduser(NEWT_DATASET_PATH))
newt_labels_df = (
    pl.read_csv(newt_dataset.labels_path).with_row_index(name="index").with_columns(pl.col("id").cast(pl.Utf8))
)

newt_pred_df = newt.predict_newt_single(
    NEWT_EMBEDDINGS_PATH,
    newt_labels_df,
    n_iter=NEWT_N_ITER,
    n_jobs=NEWT_N_JOBS,
    seed=NEWT_SEED,
).with_columns(
    pl.concat_str(pl.lit(f"{newt_dataset.images_dir}/"), pl.col("id"), pl.lit(".jpg")).alias("image_path"),
    pl.when(pl.col("y_pred") == 1).then(pl.lit("True")).otherwise(pl.lit("False")).alias("predicted_label"),
    pl.when(pl.col("y_true") == 1).then(pl.lit("True")).otherwise(pl.lit("False")).alias("actual_label"),
)

print(f"Loaded {newt_pred_df.height} NeWT test predictions")
print("NeWT labels are binary per task: True=positive class, False=negative class")
```

```{python}
newt_rng = np.random.default_rng(NEWT_SEED)

assert newt_pred_df.select(
    ((pl.col("y_pred") != pl.col("y_true")) == pl.col("is_error")).all()
).item(), "NeWT error flag mismatch: is_error is not consistent with y_pred != y_true"

newt_task_acc_df = newt_pred_df.group_by("task").agg(
    [
        ((pl.col("y_pred") == pl.col("y_true")).cast(pl.Float64)).mean().alias("accuracy"),
        pl.first("task_cluster").alias("task_cluster"),
    ]
)

newt_mistakes_df = newt_pred_df.filter(pl.col("is_error")).join(
    newt_task_acc_df.select(["task", "accuracy"]),
    on="task",
    how="left",
)

newt_task_to_candidates: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
for row in newt_mistakes_df.to_dicts():
    task = str(row["task"])
    if task not in newt_task_to_candidates:
        newt_task_to_candidates[task] = []

    newt_task_to_candidates[task].append(row)

newt_task_mistakes: list[dict] = []
for task, candidates in newt_task_to_candidates.items():
    chosen = candidates[int(newt_rng.integers(len(candidates)))]
    newt_task_mistakes.append(chosen)

assert all(bool(row["is_error"]) for row in newt_task_mistakes), "Non-mistake row leaked into NeWT task_mistakes"

newt_task_mistakes = sorted(newt_task_mistakes, key=lambda row: (row["task_cluster"], row["task"]))
print(f"Found mistakes for {len(newt_task_mistakes)}/{newt_task_acc_df.height} tasks")
# pl.DataFrame(newt_task_mistakes)
```

```{python}
def plot_newt_task_mistakes(rows: list[dict], start: int, n: int, cols: int) -> None:
    plot_mistake_grid(
        rows,
        start,
        n,
        cols,
        suptitle_template="NeWT mistakes: one per task ({start}-{end} / {total})",
        title_fn=lambda row: (
            f"{row['task_cluster']} | {row['task']}\n"
            f"Actual: {row['actual_label']} | Predicted: {row['predicted_label']}\n"
            f"Task acc: {row['accuracy']:.1%}"
        ),
        fig_scale=3.0,
        title_fontsize=7,
    )


plot_newt_task_mistakes(newt_task_mistakes, start=0, n=20, cols=5)
```

### CCT

```{python}
CCT_DATASET_PATH = "~/Datasets/CaltechCameraTraps"
CCT_EMBEDDINGS_PATH = (
    "results/cct/cct_vit_reg4_so150m_p14_ls_dino-v2-bio_0_e140_224px_crop1.0_244584_sc_embeddings.parquet"
)
CCT_SPLIT_SET = "main"
CCT_K = 20
CCT_SEED = 0
```

```{python}
cct_dataset = CaltechCameraTraps(os.path.expanduser(CCT_DATASET_PATH))
cct_metadata_df, cct_train_locations, cct_test_locations = cct._load_cct_metadata(cct_dataset, CCT_SPLIT_SET)
cct_category_data, _, cct_stats = cct._load_embeddings_with_split(CCT_EMBEDDINGS_PATH, cct_metadata_df)
cct_x_train_category, cct_y_train_category, cct_x_test_category, cct_y_test_category = cct_category_data
cct_y_pred, cct_y_true = cct.evaluate_knn(
    cct_x_train_category,
    cct_y_train_category,
    cct_x_test_category,
    cct_y_test_category,
    k=CCT_K,
    device=DEVICE,
)
print(
    f"Loaded CCT split with {cct_stats['category_train_samples']:,} train / "
    f"{cct_stats['category_test_samples']:,} test single-label images"
)
```

```{python}
with open(cct_dataset.annotations_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    cct_payload = json.load(f)

cct_id_to_file_name = {str(img["id"]): str(img["file_name"]) for img in cct_payload["images"]}
cct_category_id_to_name = {int(cat["id"]): str(cat["name"]) for cat in cct_payload["categories"]}

cct_emb_df = load_embeddings(CCT_EMBEDDINGS_PATH)
cct_test_category_df = (
    cct_metadata_df.filter(pl.col("split") == "test")
    .select(["id", "location", "label", "is_single_label"])
    .join(cct_emb_df, on="id", how="inner")
    .filter(pl.col("is_single_label"))
    .select(["id", "location", "label"])
)

assert cct_test_category_df.height == cct_y_true.shape[0], "CCT test rows are not aligned with prediction vector length"
assert np.array_equal(
    cct_test_category_df.get_column("label").to_numpy().astype(np.int_),
    cct_y_true,
), "CCT test metadata order mismatch with y_true"

cct_test_rows = cct_test_category_df.to_dicts()
for row in cct_test_rows:
    image_relpath = cct_id_to_file_name.get(str(row["id"]))
    row["image_path"] = str(cct_dataset.images_dir.joinpath(image_relpath.lstrip("/"))) if image_relpath else ""
    row["actual_name"] = cct_category_id_to_name.get(int(row["label"]), str(row["label"]))

cct_rng = np.random.default_rng(CCT_SEED)
cct_present_categories = np.unique(cct_y_true)
cct_category_acc = {
    int(category_id): float(np.mean(cct_y_pred[cct_y_true == category_id] == cct_y_true[cct_y_true == category_id]))
    for category_id in cct_present_categories
}

cct_mistakes: list[dict] = []
for category_id in cct_present_categories:
    mismatch = np.flatnonzero((cct_y_true == category_id) & (cct_y_pred != cct_y_true))
    if mismatch.size == 0:
        continue

    sample_idx = int(cct_rng.choice(mismatch))
    row = cct_test_rows[sample_idx]
    actual_id = int(row["label"])
    predicted_id = int(cct_y_pred[sample_idx])
    assert predicted_id != actual_id, "CCT sampled row is not a real mistake"

    cct_mistakes.append(
        {
            "actual_label": row["actual_name"],
            "predicted_label": cct_category_id_to_name.get(predicted_id, str(predicted_id)),
            "actual_id": actual_id,
            "predicted_id": predicted_id,
            "accuracy": cct_category_acc[int(category_id)],
            "location": row["location"],
            "image_path": row["image_path"],
        }
    )

cct_mistakes = sorted(cct_mistakes, key=lambda row: row["actual_label"])
print(f"Found mistakes for {len(cct_mistakes)}/{len(cct_present_categories)} present CCT categories")
# pl.DataFrame(cct_mistakes)
```

```{python}
def plot_cct_mistakes(rows: list[dict], start: int, n: int, cols: int) -> None:
    plot_mistake_grid(
        rows,
        start,
        n,
        cols,
        suptitle_template="CCT mistakes: one per true category ({start}-{end} / {total})",
        title_fn=lambda row: (
            f"Actual: {row['actual_label']} (id={row['actual_id']})\n"
            f"Predicted: {row['predicted_label']} (id={row['predicted_id']})\n"
            f"Location: {row['location']} | Class acc: {row['accuracy']:.1%}"
        ),
        fig_scale=3.0,
        title_fontsize=7,
    )


plot_cct_mistakes(cct_mistakes, start=0, n=20, cols=5)
```
